data.title: BJ’s Wholesale Club (NYSE: BJ) to release Q4 fiscal 2025 results on March 5, 2026. Conference call at 8:30am ET.
data.description: BJ’s Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc., a prominent membership warehouse club operator, will announce its financial results for Q4 fiscal 2025 before the market opens on Thursday, March 5, 2026. A conference call is scheduled for the same day at 8:30 a.m. ET to review the company's financial performance. The live audio webcast of the call can be found in the “Events & Presentations” section of the company’s website. BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings, Inc. is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol BJ.

data.title: Skeena Gold & Silver gets EAC for Eskay Creek project in British Columbia; Fed. Impact Assessment Act approved.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on January 27, 2026, Skeena Resources Limited (TSX: SKE, NYSE: SKE) announced the reception of its Environmental Assessment Certificate (EAC) for the Eskay Creek Gold-Silver Project situated in Northwestern British Columbia, Canada. The project, fully owned by Skeena, has been given the green light by Canada's Minister of Environment and Climate Change under the Federal Impact Assessment Act. The EAC approval marks a significant milestone for the company's operations in the region.

data.title: PEPE price could reach $0.0000070-$0.0000072 by Jan 2026, with a 30-35% rally. RSI at 44.50 signals neutral momentum.
data.description: Forecasts for PEPE indicate a possible 30-35% surge, with the price projected to reach $0.0000070-$0.0000072 by the end of January 2026. However, the current Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 44.50, signaling neutral momentum, while bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) indicators are present.

data.title: January 2026 Procurement Magazine highlights SAP, Sarantis Group, and Equans discussing AI, strategy, sustainable sourcing, and procurement leadership.
data.description: In the January 2026 edition of Procurement Magazine, SAP, Sarantis Group, and Equans are highlighted for their innovative approaches to AI strategy, sustainable sourcing, and shaping the future of procurement leadership. This issue delves into how these companies are leading the way in utilizing artificial intelligence, promoting sustainable procurement practices, and redefining traditional procurement roles. Readers will gain valuable insights into the latest trends and best practices in the procurement industry from these industry leaders.

data.title: GM Financial's net income was $460 million in Q4 2025, down from $589 million in Q3. Full year 2025 net income was $2.1 billion.
data.description: General Motors Financial Company, Inc. (GM Financial) reported a net income of $460 million for the quarter ending on December 31, 2025, down from $589 million in the previous quarter ending on September 30, 2025, but an increase from $215 million in the same quarter of 2024. The net income for the entire year of 2025 reached $2.1 billion, up from $1.9 billion in 2024. Additionally, the company recorded a $320 million impairment charge during this period.

data.title: Unitil to release Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 9, 2026. Conference call on Feb 10, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. ET.
data.description: Unitil Corporation, traded on NYSE as UTL, will be announcing its fourth quarter 2025 earnings on February 9, 2026, after the market closes. A conference call and webcast is set for February 10, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. (ET) to discuss the performance. Interested parties can access presentation materials beforehand on the Company’s Investors page at investors.unitil.com.

data.title: Tradr launched 3 new leveraged ETFs on SNDK, WDC and Lumentum capturing memory stalwarts' renaissance.
data.description: Today in New York, Tradr ETFs, a company catering to sophisticated investors and professional traders, introduced three new leveraged ETFs focused on individual stocks. These funds are listed on Cboe and signify a resurgence in the popularity of memory technology giants SNDK and WDC. This event marks a significant development in the market on Jan. 27, 2026.

data.title: Brightstar's subsidiary signs long-term contract with Wisconsin Lottery to deploy OMNIA™ solution.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, Brightstar Lottery PLC (NYSE: BRSL) revealed a significant development as its subsidiary, Brightstar Global Solutions Corporation, secured a long-term deal with the Wisconsin Lottery. The agreement will see the deployment of OMNIA™, a comprehensive lottery solution, by Brightstar. This partnership marks a milestone for the company as it aims to enhance its presence in the lottery sector. The deal is expected to drive growth and innovation, benefiting both Brightstar and the Wisconsin Lottery.

data.title: Ford and GM are in talks to provide financial lifeline to bankrupt First Brands Group by making advance payments for products, to help save the Ohio-based supplier. Talks are in final stages and deal success uncertain. Multiple automakers involved, requiring upfront payments for parts deliveries. Reported by Financial Times.
data.description: Ford Motor Co. (NYSE: F) and General Motors Co. (NYSE: GM) are currently in talks to provide financial assistance to the struggling auto parts supplier First Brands Group, which is facing bankruptcy. The proposed agreement involves the automakers making prepayments for the products they are due to receive from First Brands. This injection of funds would help First Brands maintain its operations. First Brands, based in Ohio, is a key supplier of essential components for Ford and GM, such as windshield wiper parts for Ford's F-150 pickup truck. The discussions are said to be in the final stages, as reported by the Financial Times. However, the success of the deal remains uncertain. The publication also mentioned that various automakers are engaged in similar negotiations that would require upfront payments for anticipated parts deliveries. Though the talks are nearing completion, there is still a risk of the agreement falling through, with Ford identified as particularly vulnerable in this situation.

data.title: Kimberly-Clark Corp (NASDAQ: KMB) raised dividend to $1.28 per share from $1.26, payable on April 2, 2026.
data.description: On January 27, 2026, Kimberly-Clark Corporation (NASDAQ: KMB) announced an uptick in its quarterly dividend from $1.26 to $1.28 per share. data.title: Healthgrades names top 5% of U.S. hospitals for clinical care in 2026. DENVER, Jan. 27, 2026 - recipients of America's Best Hospitals Awards announced. #healthcare
data.description: Healthgrades has acknowledged the highest performing 5% of hospitals in the United States for their exceptional clinical care. On January 27, 2026, in Denver, the leading healthcare ratings organization Healthgrades revealed the winners of the prestigious 2026 America's Best Hospitals Awards. These top hospitals represent a remarkable standard in overall clinical care and are recognized as some of the best in the nation.

data.title: Bitzero Holdings Inc. expands to 2.8 EH/s with 110 MW expansion in progress. ($BTZRF)
data.description: Bitzero Holdings Inc., a company specializing in sustainable blockchain and high-performance compute (HPC) data center solutions, has recently achieved a computing power of approximately 2.8 EH/s. The company is currently undergoing a significant expansion, with a 110 MW increase in capacity. Based in Vancouver, BC, Bitzero Holdings Inc. operates under the tickers CSE: BITZ.U, OTCQB: BTZRF, and FSE: 000. This expansion indicates the company's commitment to growing its presence in the blockchain and HPC industries, solidifying its position as a key player in the market.
